code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test09-FEB-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury with metabolic derangements. Low total CO₂ may reflect metabolic acidosis. HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or hepatic disorders.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
